items.original_text: Inspiring! X Freeze: NASA just officially unveiled their master plan for a permanent Moon Base at the lunar South Pole This is not just about flags and footprints. NASA is moving to establish an enduring, sustained human presence, and they are heavily relying on commercial innovators to build it
items.zh_text: 振奋人心！X 冻结：美国国家航空航天局（NASA）刚刚正式公布了他们在月球南极建立永久性月球基地的总体规划。这不仅仅是关于旗帜和足迹。NASA 正在迈向建立持久、可持续的人类存在，并且他们严重依赖商业创新者来建设它。

items.original_text: Grok i/o: Belgian man convicted of hate speech describes the judicial rationale for his latest conviction. I asked Gemini: Is this man's account of his conviction accurate? Gemini replied that it was grossly inaccurate. So, I copied and pasted Gemini's response, and asked Grok to reply
items.zh_text: Grok i/o：一名因仇恨言论被定罪的比利时男子描述了他最近一次定罪的司法理由。我问 Gemini：这名男子对其定罪的描述准确吗？Gemini 回答说，这严重不准确。于是，我复制粘贴了 Gemini 的回复，并让 Grok 进行回应。

items.original_text: Time to build major base on the Moon! NASA: LIVE: We're sharing the latest updates on @NASAMoonBase, our lunar habitat where astronauts will work and live. https://x.com/i/broadcasts/1OxwbbnNoLdJB
items.zh_text: 是时候在月球上建立大型基地了！NASA：直播：我们正在分享关于@NASAMoonBase的最新进展，这是我们的月球栖息地，宇航员将在此工作和生活。

items.original_text: magical experience with codex on iPad Kevin Rose: so Codex on iPad acts like a Codex mobile phone, which gives you the full desktop UI/UX. meaning, you can use your iPad to control your mac mini at home and have full screen portable development, it's really magical.
items.zh_text: 在iPad上使用Codex的神奇体验 Kevin Rose：iPad上的Codex就像一部Codex手机，它为你提供了完整的桌面UI/UX。也就是说，你可以用iPad控制家里的Mac Mini，实现全屏便携式开发，这真的很神奇。

items.original_text: Codex for analyzing and organizing your Slack: Derrick Choi: Over the weekend, I asked Codex to analyze my Slack message history and recommend a better way to organize my growing number of channels. Then I had Codex reorganize and categorize my Slack sidebar with computer use while I worked on something else. I now have an automation for
items.zh_text: 用于分析和整理Slack的Codex：Derrick Choi：上周末，我让Codex分析我的Slack消息历史，并推荐一种更好的方式来组织我不断增加的频道。然后，在我处理其他事情时，我让Codex通过计算机操作重新整理并分类了我的Slack侧边栏。现在，我有了一个自动化流程。

items.original_text: New on the Engineering Blog: The access and permissions we grant agents should evolve with their capabilities. In our own products, we set these parameters through sandboxing, which limits the scope of any potentially destructive actions. Read more: https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/how-we-contain-claude
items.zh_text: 工程博客新文章：我们授予智能体的访问和权限应随其能力的发展而演进。在我们自己的产品中，我们通过沙盒机制设置这些参数，以限制任何潜在破坏性行为的范围。阅读更多：https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/how-we-contain-claude

items.zh_text: 我们的 Gemini for Science 工具可以帮助科学家实现下一个突破。🧬

items.original_text: RT Daniel Jeffries The road to Hell is paved with closed-source citadels disguised as good intentions. The Pope is right: AI takes on the characteristics of those who build it, finance it, and regulate it. So the question is: who gets to hold the great and wonderful power of AI? If the answer is a handful of closed source companies, murkily censored, quietly surveilling every step of our lives, every private conversation, enshrined in law as 'safe' and 'open' when they're nothing but the surveillance economy squared, then all we've done is build a few modern East India Companies, digital oligarchies of the few, cloaked in the language of safety. Open Source and Open Weights are how you spread the fantastic enabling power of AI to everyone, everywhere. Permissionless innovation. Everyone gets the hammer and nails to build houses and churches and factories. The more hammers, the more widely spread, the more the decentralized genius of humankind can flourish. Everyone gets the Printing Press. The printing press singlehandedly uplifted and spread of intelligence and knowledge around the world. The more we could record all kinds of knowledge, the more we spread the ability to read, the more equal and advanced society became. Before the press, knowledge was learned by one person and passed into dust with them when they died or passed only to only a small group of students. When we only had monks in a cave copying religious texts, a closed system, it limited the spread of intelligence and limited the growth of civilization. The printing press was the single greatest invention in the history of the world because it let anyone print anything and spread knowledge throughout the whole world. AI can do the same, but only if we build the bazaar, and never let the citadel people convince the world that they're the special people who should control who gets access to intelligence while pretending they're building the bazaar. What the world needs now is more intelligence, more widely spread and more widely available. Open is the way. And it always has been. And the road to Hell was always built with walls, towers, spiked gates and moats so that only the few could enter. Pope Leo XIV: In the abstract, technology in and of itself is not a solution to humanity’s problems, just as, in and of itself, it is not inherently evil. In practice, however, technology is never neutral, because it takes on the characteristics of those who devise it, finance it, regulate it
items.zh_text: Yann LeCun 转发了 Daniel Jeffries 的推文：通往地狱的道路铺满了伪装成善意的闭源堡垒。教皇说得对：人工智能会呈现出其构建者、资助者和监管者的特征。所以问题是：谁有权掌握人工智能这伟大而神奇的力量？如果答案是少数闭源公司，它们模糊地审查，悄无声息地监视我们生活的每一步、每一次私人对话，并被法律奉为‘安全’和‘开放’，而实际上它们不过是监控经济的平方，那么我们所做的不过是建造了几个现代东印度公司，少数人的数字寡头，披着安全的外衣。开源和开放权重才是将人工智能那奇妙的赋能力量传播给每个人、每个地方的方式。无需许可的创新。每个人都得到锤子和钉子来建造房屋、教堂和工厂。锤子越多，分布越广，人类去中心化的天才就越能蓬勃发展。每个人都得到印刷机。印刷机单枪匹马地提升了知识和智慧在全世界的传播。我们能记录各种知识越多，传播阅读能力越多，社会就越平等和进步。在印刷机出现之前，知识由一个人学习，随着他们去世而化为尘土，或者只传给一小群学生。当我们只有僧侣在洞穴里抄写宗教文本时，一个封闭的系统，它限制了智慧的传播，限制了文明的成长。印刷机是世界历史上最伟大的发明，因为它让任何人印刷任何东西，并将知识传播到全世界。人工智能也能做到同样的事，但前提是我们必须建造集市，绝不能让堡垒里的人说服世界，说他们是应该控制谁获得智慧的特别之人，同时假装他们在建造集市。世界现在需要的是更多的智慧，更广泛地传播和更广泛地可用。开放是道路。而且一直如此。通往地狱的道路总是用墙壁、塔楼、尖刺大门和护城河建造的，以便只有少数人能够进入。教皇利奥十四世：抽象地说，技术本身并不是人类问题的解决方案，就像它本身并非天生邪恶一样。然而在实践中，技术从来不是中立的，因为它会呈现出设计它、资助它、监管它的人的特征。

items.original_text: RT Nathan Lambert Gemma 4 adoption numbers outpacing Qwen 3.5/3.6 for the same sized models is a big shift in the international balance of influence via open models. Interconnects: Some ideas for what comes next, May 2026 Gemini Flash 3.5, Mythos, open-closed balance, America's open-source surge, emerging power struggles and more. https://www.interconnects.ai/p/some-ideas-for-what-comes-next-may
items.zh_text: 转发 Nathan Lambert：Gemma 4 的采用率超过同等规模的 Qwen 3.5/3.6，这标志着通过开放模型实现的国际影响力平衡发生了重大转变。Interconnects：关于下一步的一些想法，2026 年 5 月 Gemini Flash 3.5、Mythos、开放与封闭的平衡、美国的开源浪潮、新兴的权力斗争等。https://www.interconnects.ai/p/some-ideas-for-what-comes-next-may

items.original_text: All plot lines inevitably converge. Christopher F. Rufo ⚔️: California is spending millions of dollars sending contractors into the farm fields, trying to persuade undocumented migrants to sign up for free solar panels—all funded by taxpayers.
items.zh_text: 所有故事线终将交汇。Christopher F. Rufo ⚔️：加州正花费数百万美元派遣承包商进入农田，试图说服无证移民注册免费太阳能板——全部由纳税人买单。

items.original_text: Honestly this never would have even occurred to me. Christopher F. Rufo ⚔️: SCOOP: California has spent $49 million on a program that gives free solar panels to illegal aliens. The state claims that its "farmworker weatherization" initiative fights climate change—but we traced the funds and found signals of waste and corruption. https://www.city-journal.org/article/california-free-solar-panels-illegal-immigrants
items.zh_text: 老实说，我根本没想到这一点。Christopher F. Rufo ⚔️：独家消息：加州已花费4900万美元用于一项向非法移民提供免费太阳能电池板的计划。该州声称其“农场工人防寒保暖”倡议旨在应对气候变化——但我们追踪了资金，发现了浪费和腐败的迹象。https://www.city-journal.org/article/california-free-solar-panels-illegal-immigrants

items.original_text: RT Christopher F. Rufo ⚔️ California is spending millions of dollars sending contractors into the farm fields, trying to persuade undocumented migrants to sign up for free solar panels—all funded by taxpayers. City Journal: @christopherrufo A contractor drove hundreds of miles a day trying to get illegal aliens to sign up and had to convince them that it wasn't a scam. Incredible stuff.
items.zh_text: Marc Andreessen 转发 Christopher F. Rufo ⚔️ 加州正在花费数百万美元派遣承包商进入农田，试图说服无证移民注册免费太阳能电池板——全部由纳税人资助。城市杂志：@christopherrufo 一名承包商每天驱车数百英里，试图让非法移民注册，并不得不说服他们这不是骗局。令人难以置信。

items.original_text: Codex for finding space on your laptop: BOOTOSHI 👑: i had codex audit my entire macbook to see how much space we can save and it's found 500 GB to save, AWESOME prompt was: "do a FULL read only analysis on my Macbook to help me optimize storage" note: why tf is there a codex-tui.log file that is 116gb ??????? WHAT ????
items.zh_text: 用 Codex 清理笔记本电脑空间：BOOTOSHI 👑：我让 Codex 审计了我的整个 MacBook，看看能节省多少空间，结果发现可以节省 500 GB，太棒了。提示词是：“对我的 MacBook 进行完全的只读分析，帮助我优化存储”。注意：为什么 tf 会有一个 116GB 的 codex-tui.log 文件？？？？？什么？？？？

items.original_text: Anthropic co-founder Chris Olah was invited to speak at today's presentation of Pope Leo XIV's encyclical "Magnifica humanitas." Read the full text of his remarks: https://www.anthropic.com/news/chris-olah-pope-leo-encyclical
items.zh_text: Anthropic 联合创始人 Chris Olah 受邀在教皇利奥十四世通谕《Magnifica humanitas》的今日发布仪式上发表演讲。阅读他的演讲全文：https://www.anthropic.com/news/chris-olah-pope-leo-encyclical

items.original_text: RT Pushmeet Kohli AI agents are advancing research-level math. 🚀 I’m thrilled to share @GoogleDeepMind’s AlphaProof Nexus - an agentic framework for formal proof search powered by Gemini. When applied to a set of open formal math problems, our agent autonomously solved: ✅ 9 open Erdős problems (including two open for 56 years!) ✅ 44 Online Encyclopedia of Integer Sequences (OEIS) problems ✅ A 15-year-old open problem in algebraic geometry ✅ A 7-year-old open question in min-max optimization We are collaborating with mathematicians across disciplines - from combinatorics and graph theory to quantum optics. Ultimately, these results show the massive potential of even simple agentic loops powered by Gemini. Read the paper here: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.22763v1
items.zh_text: 谷歌DeepMind：RT Pushmeet Kohli AI智能体正在推进研究级数学。🚀 我很激动地分享@GoogleDeepMind的AlphaProof Nexus——一个由Gemini驱动的形式化证明搜索智能体框架。当应用于一组开放的形式化数学问题时，我们的智能体自主解决了：✅ 9个开放的Erdős问题（其中两个已开放56年！）✅ 44个在线整数序列百科全书（OEIS）问题 ✅ 一个存在15年的代数几何开放问题 ✅ 一个存在7年的极小极大优化开放问题。我们正在与跨学科的数学家合作——从组合学和图论到量子光学。最终，这些结果展示了即使是由Gemini驱动的简单智能体循环的巨大潜力。在此阅读论文：https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.22763v1

items.original_text: RT Basile Terver 📢 Accepted to TMLR, with reproducibility certification 🏅 v2 of our JEPA-WM study (arXiv:2512.24497) is out, with new data-scaling experiments, a Lipschitz analysis of multistep rollout training, and extended discussions. Recap + what's new 👇 w/ @JimmyTYYang1, Jean Ponce, @AdrienBardes, @ylecun Basile Terver: My first PhD paper is out! 🎓 "What Drives Success in Physical Planning with Joint-Embedding Predictive World Models?" tl:dr: JEPA-WMs for robotics: learn dynamics on top of visual encoders, optimize actions towards goal 👇 w/ @JimmyTYYang1, Jean Ponce, @AdrienBardes, @ylecun
items.zh_text: Yann LeCun：转发 Basile Terver 📢 我们的 JEPA-WM 研究（arXiv:2512.24497）第二版已被 TMLR 接收，并获得可重复性认证 🏅。新版本包含新的数据缩放实验、多步 rollout 训练的 Lipschitz 分析，以及扩展的讨论。回顾 + 新内容 👇 与 @JimmyTYYang1、Jean Ponce、@AdrienBardes、@ylecun 合作。Basile Terver：我的第一篇博士论文发表了！🎓 《什么推动了联合嵌入预测世界模型在物理规划中的成功？》摘要：用于机器人学的 JEPA-WM：在视觉编码器之上学习动力学，优化动作以达成目标 👇 与 @JimmyTYYang1、Jean Ponce、@AdrienBardes、@ylecun 合作。

items.original_text: RT Pushmeet Kohli AI agents are advancing research-level math. 🚀 I’m thrilled to share @GoogleDeepMind’s AlphaProof Nexus - an agentic framework for formal proof search powered by Gemini. When applied to a set of open formal math problems, our agent autonomously solved: ✅ 9 open Erdős problems (including two open for 56 years!) ✅ 44 Online Encyclopedia of Integer Sequences (OEIS) problems ✅ A 15-year-old open problem in algebraic geometry ✅ A 7-year-old open question in min-max optimization We are collaborating with mathematicians across disciplines - from combinatorics and graph theory to quantum optics. Ultimately, these results show the massive potential of even simple agentic loops powered by Gemini. Read the paper here: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.22763v1
items.zh_text: RT Pushmeet Kohli：AI智能体正在推进研究级数学。🚀 我很激动地分享@GoogleDeepMind的AlphaProof Nexus——一个由Gemini驱动的形式化证明搜索智能体框架。当应用于一组开放形式化数学问题时，我们的智能体自主解决了：✅ 9个开放Erdős问题（包括两个已开放56年的问题！）✅ 44个在线整数序列百科全书（OEIS）问题 ✅ 一个存在15年的代数几何开放问题 ✅ 一个存在7年的极小极大优化开放问题。我们正在与跨学科的数学家合作——从组合学、图论到量子光学。最终，这些结果展示了即使是由Gemini驱动的简单智能体循环的巨大潜力。在此阅读论文：https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.22763v1

items.original_text: We will continue to see layoffs from companies with large eng teams Engineers are producing 10-100x the code they used to.... This is causing a lot of instability in companies with large code bases and teams The status-quo is simply not sustainable

items.original_text: new codex ships today! OpenAI Developers: It’s Codex Thursday, and yes, we have updates for you. First up: Appshots, a new way to bring the context of what you’re working on into Codex. On your Mac, press Command-Command to attach your app window to a Codex thread. Codex gets both a screenshot and text from the window,
items.zh_text: 新的 Codex 今天发布！OpenAI 开发者们：今天是 Codex 星期四，没错，我们为你们带来了更新。首先是：Appshots，一种将你正在处理的内容上下文带入 Codex 的新方式。在你的 Mac 上，按下 Command-Command 键，将你的应用窗口附加到 Codex 线程中。Codex 会从窗口中获取截图和文本。

items.original_text: Introducing OpenAI Guaranteed Capacity: a new offering that enables customers to guarantee long-term access to OpenAI compute. We’ve made long-term investments in infrastructure, partnerships, and capacity planning to help customers scale reliably. Now, Guaranteed Capacity helps customers plan ahead for critical workloads in a compute-constrained world. http://openai.com/guaranteed-capacity
items.zh_text: OpenAI 推出保障容量服务：一项新服务，让客户能够确保长期使用 OpenAI 的计算资源。我们在基础设施、合作伙伴关系和容量规划方面进行了长期投资，以帮助客户可靠地扩展规模。如今，保障容量服务帮助客户在计算资源受限的环境中，提前规划关键工作负载。

items.original_text: RT Sam Altman if this tweet gets 1 like, tibo will reset codex rate limits

items.original_text: We’re adding new ways for people to identify AI-generated images and understand where they came from. In addition to C2PA Content Credentials, images now also contain a SynthID watermark, and can be identified using a public verification tool to check whether an image was made by OpenAI products. https://openai.com/index/advancing-content-provenance/
items.zh_text: 我们正在增加新的方式，让人们能够识别AI生成的图像并了解其来源。除了C2PA内容凭证外，图像现在还包含SynthID水印，并且可以使用公开验证工具来检查图像是否由OpenAI产品生成。

items.original_text: Personal update: I've joined Anthropic. I think the next few years at the frontier of LLMs will be especially formative. I am very excited to join the team here and get back to R&D. I remain deeply passionate about education and plan to resume my work on it in time.
items.zh_text: 个人动态：我已加入Anthropic。我认为未来几年在大语言模型前沿领域将尤为关键。我非常兴奋能加入这里的团队并重返研发工作。我依然对教育充满热忱，并计划适时重启相关事业。

WITHDRAW YOUR FAKE STATEMENT, MASSIE, RIGHT NOW! President DONALD J. TRUMP
items.zh_text: 可恶的国会议员托马斯·马西发布了一份多年前我给他的旧背书，那是在我发现他是我国历史上最糟糕的国会议员之前很久的事了。我背书了埃德·加雷因，一位真正的美国爱国者，马西对此心知肚明，所以他发布的声明是欺诈性的，就像他本人一样欺诈。马西，立即撤回你的虚假声明！唐纳德·J·特朗普总统

items.original_text: Over the past few months, we've been holding dialogues with scholars, philosophers, clergy, and ethicists on the questions AI raises—starting with how good character forms. Read more about how we’re widening the conversation on frontier AI: https://www.anthropic.com/news/widening-conversation-ai
items.zh_text: 过去几个月里，我们与学者、哲学家、神职人员和伦理学家就人工智能引发的问题展开了对话——从良好品格如何形成开始。了解更多关于我们如何拓宽前沿人工智能对话的信息：https://www.anthropic.com/news/widening-conversation-ai

items.original_text: Rho cut weekly meeting time by 90% with Perplexity Computer. Computer checks Slack, Notion, Jira, Figma, and Google Docs, then flags missing tasks and changes the team needs to see. 120 work hours saved during a 12-week project. Read the customer story: https://www.perplexity.ai/enterprise/customers/rho
items.zh_text: Rho 借助 Perplexity Computer 将每周会议时间减少了 90%。Computer 会检查 Slack、Notion、Jira、Figma 和 Google Docs，然后标记团队需要关注的遗漏任务和变更。在一个 12 周的项目中节省了 120 个工时。阅读客户案例：https://www.perplexity.ai/enterprise/customers/rho

items.original_text: Our new multi-model agentic security system brings together more than 100 specialized agents across frontier and custom models to find exploitable bugs, delivering top performance on the CyberGym benchmark. We used it ahead of Patch Tuesday to help find and fix 16 vulnerabilities. Today we’re announcing that customers can sign up to test it in private preview. https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2026/05/12/defense-at-ai-speed-microsofts-new-multi-model-agentic-security-system-tops-leading-industry-benchmark/?v=1
items.zh_text: 我们的新型多模型自主安全系统整合了前沿模型和定制模型中的100多个专业智能体，用于发现可利用漏洞，在CyberGym基准测试中取得了顶级性能。我们在补丁星期二之前使用该系统帮助发现并修复了16个漏洞。今天，我们宣布客户可以注册在私人预览中测试该系统。

items.original_text: PayPal runs 74,000 weekly tasks in Perplexity Enterprise. Teams use it for model validation, channel performance, market trend research, competitive intelligence, and product analysis. Read the customer story: https://www.perplexity.ai/enterprise/customers/paypal
items.zh_text: PayPal 在 Perplexity Enterprise 上每周运行 74,000 个任务。团队将其用于模型验证、渠道绩效、市场趋势研究、竞争情报和产品分析。阅读客户案例：https://www.perplexity.ai/enterprise/customers/paypal

items.original_text: This works really well btw, at the end of your query ask your LLM to "structure your response as HTML", then view the generated file in your browser. I've also had some success asking the LLM to present its output as slideshows, etc. More generally, imo audio is the human-preferred input to AIs but vision (images/animations/video) is the preferred output from them. Around a ~third of our brains are a massively parallel processor dedicated to vision, it is the 10-lane superhighway of information into brain. As AI improves, I think we'll see a progression that takes advantage: 1) raw text (hard/effortful to read) 2) markdown (bold, italic, headings, tables, a bit easier on the eyes) <-- current default 3) HTML (still procedural with underlying code, but a lot more flexibility on the graphics, layout, even interactivity) <-- early but forming new good default ...4,5,6,... n) interactive neural videos/simulations Imo the extrapolation (though the technology doesn't exist just yet) ends in some kind of interactive videos generated directly by a diffusion neural net. Many open questions as to how exact/procedural "Software 1.0" artifacts (e.g. interactive simulations) may be woven together with neural artifacts (diffusion grids), but generally something in the direction of the recently viral https://x.com/zan2434/status/2046982383430496444 There are also improvements necessary and pending at the input. Audio nor text nor video alone are not enough, e.g. I feel a need to point/gesture to things on the screen, similar to all the things you would do with a person physically next to you and your computer screen. TLDR The input/output mind meld between humans and AIs is ongoing and there is a lot of work to do and significant progress to be made, way before jumping all the way into neuralink-esque BCIs and all that. For what's worth exploring at the current stage, hot tip try ask for HTML. Thariq: http://x.com/i/article/2052796100608974848
items.zh_text: 顺便说一句，这个方法效果很好：在提问结束时，让你的大语言模型“以 HTML 格式组织回答”，然后在浏览器中查看生成的文件。我还尝试过让大语言模型以幻灯片等形式呈现输出，也取得了一些成功。更广泛地说，我认为音频是人类偏好的 AI 输入方式，而视觉（图像/动画/视频）则是偏好的输出方式。我们大脑中大约三分之一的部分是一个专门处理视觉的大规模并行处理器，它是信息进入大脑的 10 车道超级高速公路。随着 AI 的进步，我认为我们会看到一系列利用这一优势的演进：1）纯文本（阅读起来费力/费劲）2）Markdown（粗体、斜体、标题、表格，对眼睛稍微友好一些）<-- 当前默认 3）HTML（仍然有底层代码的过程性，但在图形、布局甚至交互性上灵活得多）<-- 早期但正在形成新的良好默认值 ...4,5,6,... n）交互式神经视频/模拟。我认为外推（尽管技术尚未存在）最终会走向由扩散神经网络直接生成的某种交互式视频。关于“软件 1.0”制品（例如交互式模拟）如何与神经制品（扩散网格）精确/过程性地交织在一起，还有许多悬而未决的问题，但总体方向类似于最近爆火的 https://x.com/zan2434/status/2046982383430496444。在输入方面也有必要且待进行的改进。音频、文本或视频单独都不够，例如，我觉得需要指向/手势屏幕上的东西，就像你与一个物理上在你旁边的人以及你的电脑屏幕所做的一切。TLDR：人类与 AI 之间的输入/输出思维融合正在进行中，还有很多工作要做，并且需要取得重大进展，远在直接跳入 Neuralink 式的脑机接口等之前。就当前阶段值得探索的内容而言，一个小窍门是尝试要求 HTML。Thariq：http://x.com/i/article/2052796100608974848
items.media_note: 该动态可能包含 X 原生媒体，当前 RSS 未提供视频文件，请打开原帖观看。

items.original_text: Excel has quietly been Turing complete for a long time. Nice to see it now edging toward "AI complete"—SGD, attention, next-token prediction… all in cells. Austin Henley: Excel Copilot one-shotted a tiny GPT-style language model for me inside a spreadsheet: embeddings, causal attention, weights trained with stochastic gradient descent, next-token prediction, and a live slider to watch it learn.
items.zh_text: Excel 早已悄然图灵完备。如今它正迈向“AI 完备”——随机梯度下降、注意力机制、下一词元预测……全在单元格里实现，令人欣喜。Austin Henley：Excel Copilot 在电子表格中为我一次性构建了一个微型的 GPT 风格语言模型：嵌入层、因果注意力、通过随机梯度下降训练的权重、下一词元预测，以及一个实时滑块来观察学习过程。

items.original_text: RT Jeff Hollan Here's another reason why @Microsoft Foundry is the BEST platform in the world to build and run your agents: Our durable, long running agents come with Toolbox 🔨 Toolbox allowed me to reduce input tokens 90%(!!) and significantly simplify the amount of code needed Check out the demo below and see how you can bring hundreds of tools through a single MCP endpoint -- all running in Foundry at cloud scale! ☁️
items.zh_text: 转 Jeff Hollan：@Microsoft Foundry 是全球构建和运行智能体的最佳平台，还有一个原因：我们的持久化、长时间运行的智能体配备了工具箱 🔨 工具箱让我减少了 90%（！！）的输入令牌，并显著简化了所需代码量。查看下面的演示，了解如何通过单个 MCP 端点引入数百个工具——全部在 Foundry 中以云规模运行！☁️

items.original_text: This is the the quote I've been citing a lot recently. kache: you can outsource your thinking but you cannot outsource your understanding
items.zh_text: 这是我最近经常引用的一句话。kache：你可以外包你的思考，但你不能外包你的理解。
